paper_title: Divergent Rules for Pollen and Nectar Foraging Bumblebees – A Laboratory Study with Artificial Flowers Offering Diluted Nectar Substitute and Pollen Surrogate

methods.description: This method involves monitoring the nectar and pollen-foraging activities of individually marked bumblebee foragers over a period of 41 days to understand their foraging behavior.

steps.description: Monitor the nectar and pollen-foraging activities in the feeding box of 48 individually marked foragers of the first colony for 41 days.
steps.searchable_summary: monitor foraging activities of marked bumblebees

steps.description: Count the foraging flights of the 48 bumblebee workers for 1 hour on 5 different days.
Adjust the amount of sugar solution and pollen offered based on colony storage levels to prevent overstocking.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust food supply based on storage levels

methods.method_name: Collective Foraging on Resources Varying in Quality
methods.description: This method examines the collective foraging behavior of a bumblebee colony when offered resources of varying quality to determine their preferences.

steps.description: Offer 5 nectar feeders and 5 pollen feeders simultaneously in the feeding box from 11:00 for a varying length of time over one month.
steps.searchable_summary: offer feeders with varying resource quality

steps.description: Measure the amount of sugar solution and pollen surrogate collected from each feeder at different points in time.
Analyze whether bumblebees prefer the highest resource quality by interrupting foraging when the first feeder is emptied.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze preference for highest resource quality

methods.method_name: Individual Foraging on Resources Varying in Quality and Quantity
methods.description: This method investigates individual bumblebee foraging behavior on artificial flowers with varying resource quality and quantity to understand their decision-making process.

steps.description: Allow individual bumblebee workers to forage on a vertical artificial 'flower meadow' with 42 artificial flowers.
steps.searchable_summary: allow bumblebees to forage on artificial flowers

steps.description: Record the number of flowers of each type visited by each bumblebee during the trial.
Calculate the amount of rewards collected by measuring the sugar solution left in pipette tips and weighing pollen from corbiculae.
